title: Elegy Catalog Entry
description: Marketplace and catalog readiness metadata for a publishable Elegy plugin package. This is a separate discovery/index layer from the portable package authority (elegy-plugin-package/v1).

packageId.description: Stable package identifier matching the plugin package identity.

version.description: Semantic or calendar version string for this catalog entry.

archiveUrl.description: Download URL for the published package archive.

algorithm.description: Hash algorithm used for the digest.

digest.description: Cryptographic digest of the package archive.

versionRange.description: Supported version range for the target host.

license.description: SPDX license identifier or license file reference.

sourceRef.description: Git ref (tag or branch) the package was built from.

sourceCommit.description: Full source commit SHA this catalog entry was generated from.

verificationStatus.description: Verification status of the published package against its provenance claims.

verifiedAtUtc.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful verification.

verifier.description: Identifier of the verifier (e.g. CI workflow, maintainer).

evidenceUri.description: URL to verification evidence (e.g. CI run, attestation log).

verificationEvidence.description: Evidence and metadata from the last verification pass.

provenanceRef.description: Path or URI to provenance attestation.

signatureRefs.description: Paths or URIs to cryptographic signatures over the package archive. Each entry MUST reference a verifiable signature.

ref.description: Path or URI to the signature file.

identity.description: Signer identity (e.g. key fingerprint, certificate subject).

publishedAtUtc.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this catalog entry was published.

catalogId.description: Identifier of the catalog or marketplace this entry targets.

entryKind.description: Stability classification for this catalog entry.

replacesEntryId.description: Catalog entry ID this entry supersedes.

catalogMetadata.description: Catalog-level routing metadata (not part of the package contract).
